file_name: VAPOTHERM, INC. - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 Invoices and Payment Terms. On delivery by Medica of a shipment of Cartridges in accordance with Section 3.3, Medica shall issue to Vapotherm an invoice for that shipment stating a price consistent with the terms of this Agreement. Vapotherm shall pay each such invoice in full within [* * *] Calendar Days from the date of invoice, unless Vapotherm has rejected the shipment in question in accordance with Section 4.2.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 Invoices and Payment Terms. On delivery by Medica of a shipment of Cartridges in accordance with Section 3.3, Medica shall issue to Vapotherm an invoice for that shipment stating a price consistent with the terms of this Agreement. Vapotherm shall pay each such invoice in full within [* * *] Calendar Days from the date of invoice, unless Vapotherm has rejected the shipment in question in accordance with Section 4.2. However, for the first three months of this agreement, payment is due within 15 calendar days.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying two different payment deadlines. The original clause states that payment is due within [* * *] calendar days, but the added sentence indicates a 15-day payment term for the first three months. This creates uncertainty about which payment deadline applies during the initial period of the agreement. This is contrary to Section 3.8, which talks about 'delay of payment beyond [* * *] days from invoice date'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.8 Delay in Payment. Upon delay of payment beyond [* * *] days from invoice date, Medica at its' sole discretion may levy an increase to the net transfer price a [* * *]% per week to a maximum of [* * *]%.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by establishing two distinct payment deadlines. Initially, Vapotherm has '[* * *] Calendar Days' to pay invoices, but the amended clause mandates a 15-day payment period for the first three months. This directly conflicts with Section 3.8, which specifies consequences for 'delay of payment beyond [* * *] days from invoice date.' The altered text creates uncertainty about the applicable payment term and its associated consequences during the initial three months, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.8 Delay in Payment. Upon delay of payment beyond [* * *] days from invoice date, Medica at its' sole discretion may levy an increase to the net transfer price a [* * *]% per week to a maximum of [* * *]%.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.8 Delay in Payment. Upon delay of payment beyond [* * *] days from invoice date, Medica at its' sole discretion may levy an increase to the net transfer price a [* * *]% per week to a maximum of [* * *]%. However, no interest will be charged on late payments if the delay is due to a dispute over the invoice amount.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that no interest will be charged on late payments if the delay stems from a dispute over the invoice amount. This contradicts the original clause, which implies that interest *may* be charged on *all* late payments, regardless of the reason for the delay. The original text gives Medica the 'sole discretion' to levy interest, but the added exception limits this discretion, creating ambiguity and the potential for disagreement over whether interest should be applied. This also goes against section 3.7 which mentions 'Vapotherm shall pay each such invoice in full within [* * *] Calendar Days from the date of invoice'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 Invoices and Payment Terms. On delivery by Medica of a shipment of Cartridges in accordance with Section 3.3, Medica shall issue to Vapotherm an invoice for that shipment stating a price consistent with the terms of this Agreement. Vapotherm shall pay each such invoice in full within [* * *] Calendar Days from the date of invoice, unless Vapotherm has rejected the shipment in question in accordance with Section 4.2.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to the payment terms outlined in section 3.7. Section 3.7 states 'Vapotherm shall pay each such invoice in full within [* * *] Calendar Days from the date of invoice'. The addition to section 3.8, stating 'However, no interest will be charged on late payments if the delay is due to a dispute over the invoice amount', directly contradicts this by allowing for delayed payment without penalty under certain circumstances, creating an inconsistency regarding the obligation to pay 'in full' and the consequences of late payment. This constitutes an in-text contradiction as per the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 13.3 Effect of Termination. (a) Upon any termination (including expiration) of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all documents and other tangible items to it or its employees or agents have received or created pursuant to this Agreement pertaining, referring, or relating to Confidential Information of the other party. (b) Termination of this Agreement will not affect rights and obligations of either party that may have accrued prior to the date of termination or any other obligation contained in Section 5.5, 6.3, 6.4, 8.1, 8.3, Article 9, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 10.4, 10.5(b), 10.6, Article 12, Article 13, and Sections 14.3, 14.4, and 14.5. All rights and obligation decay after 2 (two) years from termination or expiration. (c) Upon any termination (including expiration) of this Agreement, Vapotherm shall pay to Medica, and Medica shall pay to Vapotherm, all amounts payable up to the date of termination but not yet paid. (d) The termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its responsibility to comply in all material respects with any statutory or regulatory requirements associated with the System and/or the Cartridges.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.3 Effect of Termination. (a) Upon any termination (including expiration) of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all documents and other tangible items to it or its employees or agents have received or created pursuant to this Agreement pertaining, referring, or relating to Confidential Information of the other party. (b) Termination of this Agreement will not affect rights and obligations of either party that may have accrued prior to the date of termination or any other obligation contained in Section 5.5, 6.3, 6.4, 8.1, 8.3, Article 9, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 10.4, 10.5(b), 10.6, Article 12, Article 13, and Sections 14.3, 14.4, and 14.5. All rights and obligation decay after 2 (two) years from termination or expiration. However, obligations related to Intellectual Property survive indefinitely. (c) Upon any termination (including expiration) of this Agreement, Vapotherm shall pay to Medica, and Medica shall pay to Vapotherm, all amounts payable up to the date of termination but not yet paid. (d) The termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its responsibility to comply in all material respects with any statutory or regulatory requirements associated with the System and/or the Cartridges.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence, 'However, obligations related to Intellectual Property survive indefinitely,' creates an in-text contradiction with the existing clause, 'All rights and obligation decay after 2 (two) years from termination or expiration.' The original clause states that *all* rights and obligations decay after two years, but the added sentence states that obligations related to Intellectual Property survive indefinitely. This creates uncertainty and ambiguity about the duration of obligations regarding Intellectual Property after termination, contradicting the original clause and requiring legal clarification to determine which term should prevail.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.3 Effect of Termination. (a) Upon any termination (including expiration) of this Agreement, each party shall return to the other party all documents and other tangible items to it or its employees or agents have received or created pursuant to this Agreement pertaining, referring, or relating to Confidential Information of the other party. (b) Termination of this Agreement will not affect rights and obligations of either party that may have accrued prior to the date of termination or any other obligation contained in Section 5.5, 6.3, 6.4, 8.1, 8.3, Article 9, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 10.4, 10.5(b), 10.6, Article 12, Article 13, and Sections 14.3, 14.4, and 14.5. All rights and obligation decay after 2 (two) years from termination or expiration. However, obligations related to Intellectual Property survive indefinitely. (c) Upon any termination (including expiration) of this Agreement, Vapotherm shall pay to Medica, and Medica shall pay to Vapotherm, all amounts payable up to the date of termination but not yet paid. (d) The termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its responsibility to comply in all material respects with any statutory or regulatory requirements associated with the System and/or the Cartridges.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original clause 'All rights and obligation decay after 2 (two) years from termination or expiration' explicitly states that *all* rights and obligations decay after two years. The added sentence 'However, obligations related to Intellectual Property survive indefinitely' creates a clear exception for intellectual property, directly contradicting the original all-encompassing statement. This creates a strong and undeniable conflict within the document regarding the duration of obligations related to intellectual property, which is an inconsistency as defined in the instructions and creates uncertainty and ambiguity about the duration of obligations regarding Intellectual Property after termination, contradicting the original clause.
